data.title: India's Global Health gains; Jefferies sees losses easing at Noida hospital
data.description:
** Shares of India's Global Health jump 2.77% to 1111.25 rupees

** Jefferies ("buy", PT: 1,440 rupees) says losses at co's Noida hospital appear to have peaked

** Brokerage estimates cumulative Noida losses at ~8% of EBITDA so far, easing to ~2% by FY27 as patient numbers rise

** Says steady growth at mature hospitals and ramp-up at Lucknow and Patna should support medium-term earnings and margins

** Estimates ~20% revenue and ~21% EBITDA growth over FY25–FY28

** GLOH rated "buy" on average by 13 analysts, media PT: 1,250 rupees - LSEG data

** YTD, stock down ~6.2%

data.title: Nokian Tyres' loss narrows more than expected, shares rise
data.description:
Nokian Tyres reported a narrower loss than the market had expected on Wednesday, sending its shares higher, even as the Finnish tyre maker warned that market uncertainty was set to persist amid geopolitical tensions.

Nokian's adjusted operating loss narrowed to 4.3 million euros ($5.1 million) in the first three months of 2026, from 18.5 million euros a year ago. Analysts were expecting an average loss of 6.7 million euros in a company-provided poll.

The company's shares rose 5% following the results, having traded flat before the publication.

The U.S-Israeli war with Iran is driving growing uncertainty in the already troubled automotive industry. Tyre makers may also take a more direct hit from spiking oil prices, as crude oil derivatives are used as a raw material to make synthetic rubber for their tyres.

data.title: Otis reports Q1 2026: Revenue $3.57B, GAAP EPS $0.87, adjusted EPS $0.89
data.description:
Otis reported first-quarter 2026 net sales of $3.57 billion, up 6% year-over-year (organic sales +1%), with GAAP diluted earnings per share of $0.87 and adjusted diluted EPS of $0.89. GAAP operating profit rose to $539 million while adjusted operating profit was $550 million; operating cash flow totaled $413 million and adjusted free cash flow was $272 million. The company cited Service-led growth—notably repair and modernization order momentum—alongside near-term Service margin pressures from cost and investment actions.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $3,566 million for the quarter, up 6% versus prior year (organic sales +1%).
  • GAAP operating profit: $539 million; GAAP operating profit margin: 15.1% (up 280 bps year-over-year).
  • Adjusted operating profit: $550 million; adjusted operating profit margin: 15.4% (down 130 bps year-over-year).
  • Net income attributable to Otis: $340 million; GAAP diluted EPS: $0.87; adjusted diluted EPS: $0.89.
  • Cash flow: operating cash flow $413 million; free cash flow $380 million; adjusted free cash flow $272 million.

Business Highlights

  • Service segment drove growth: Service net sales totaled $2,417 million, up 11% year-over-year with organic Service sales up 5%; repair grew strongly.
  • Repair and modernization momentum: Repair net sales up 16% (organic ≈10%); modernization orders rose 11% at constant currency and modernization backlog increased ~30% at constant currency.
  • New Equipment segment: Net sales of $1,149 million, down 1% year-over-year (organic -5%); New Equipment orders up 1% at constant currency with backlog up 3% at constant currency.
  • Operational pressures and actions: Service margins faced near-term headwinds from higher labor and material costs, investments to support growth, tariff impacts, and shipment delays tied to geopolitical issues; company is focused on execution, pricing, and cost efficiency.
  • Capital allocation & strategic activity: Repurchased approximately $400 million of shares and announced a majority investment in WeMaintain, a digitally native, AI-enabled elevator service provider.

Original SEC Filing: Otis Worldwide Corp [ OTIS ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: HIVE Digital Technologies Prices $115 Million 0% Exchangeable Notes, Adds $19.8 Million Capped Calls
data.description:
HIVE Digital Technologies priced a $115 million offering of 0% exchangeable senior notes due 2031, issued by a wholly owned subsidiary and guaranteed by HIVE. The notes are exchangeable at an initial rate of 389.5029 shares per $1,000 (about $2.57 per share) and can be settled in cash, stock, or both, with investor put rights in 2029 and conditional issuer redemption beginning in 2029. To mitigate dilution and manage potential cash outlays upon exchanges, HIVE also entered into capped call transactions on April 16 and 17, 2026, costing approximately $19.8 million and featuring a $4.92 initial cap price. The moves provide zero-coupon financing and help protect shareholders against dilution from conversions.

Agreement 1: HIVE Digital Technologies Prices $115 Million 0% Exchangeable Notes Due 2031

  • Agreement type: Indenture for 0% exchangeable senior notes due 2031
  • Counterparty: U.S. Bank Trust Company, as trustee, and initial purchasers
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 21 2026 / Apr 21 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through Apr 15 2031
  • Reason: Raise capital with flexible, zero-coupon financing

Agreement 2: HIVE Digital Technologies Enters $19.8 Million Capped Calls to Mitigate Dilution, Cap at $4.92

  • Agreement type: Capped call transactions on common shares
  • Counterparty: Option counterparties (certain financial institutions)
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 16 2026 / Apr 16 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through Apr 15 2031
  • Reason: Reduce potential dilution from note exchanges

Original SEC Filing: HIVE Digital Technologies Ltd. [ HIVE ] - 8-K - Apr. 21, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Elevance Health reports 1Q26 revenue $49.5B, adjusted diluted EPS $12.58; raises FY26 adjusted EPS to $26.75
data.description:
Elevance Health reported first-quarter 2026 results with operating revenue of $49.5 billion and adjusted diluted EPS of $12.58, driven by strong operating results and about $1.00 per share of non-recurring investment income. GAAP diluted EPS was $8.00 for the quarter; the company raised full-year 2026 adjusted diluted EPS guidance to at least $26.75 and reaffirmed operating cash flow of at least $5.5 billion. Management cited improving claims experience, actions to reduce medical costs and increased visibility supporting the outlook.

Financial Highlights

  • Operating revenue: $49.5 billion for the three months ended March 31, 2026 (up 1.5% year-over-year).
  • Operating gain: $2.1 billion for the quarter; adjusted operating gain: $3.2 billion.
  • GAAP shareholders' net income: $1,764 million; GAAP diluted EPS: $8.00.
  • Adjusted shareholders' net income: $2,772 million; adjusted diluted EPS: $12.58.
  • Full-year 2026 guidance: GAAP diluted EPS at least $19.85; adjusted diluted EPS raised to at least $26.75. Operating cash flow reaffirmed at least $5.5 billion.

Business Highlights

  • Health Benefits segment operating revenue increased to $42.5 billion (up 2.6% year-over-year) driven primarily by higher premium yields and growth in CarelonRx product revenue.
  • Carelon combined revenue growth: Carelon operating revenue noted at $18.0 billion, with CarelonRx and Carelon Services expanding—CarelonRx revenue $10.6 billion and Carelon Services $7.4 billion.
  • Membership mix: Total medical membership approximately 45.4 million as of March 31, 2026, with commercial fee-based expansion offsetting declines in Medicare Advantage and Medicaid risk membership.
  • Operational metrics: Benefit expense ratio 86.8% (up 40 bps) reflecting elevated Medicaid medical cost trends; days in claims payable 46.6 as of March 31, 2026.
  • Corporate actions: Recorded a $935 million accrual for the Company's current best estimate of potential exposure related to a CMS notice and a $129 million business optimization charge; returned $1.5 billion of capital to shareholders in Q1 via share repurchases and dividends.

Original SEC Filing: Elevance Health, Inc. [ ELV ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Brand Engagement Network Plans Up To $1 Million Investment and Collaboration With Accelevate Solutions
data.description:
Brand Engagement Network entered into a letter agreement with Accelevate Solutions outlining a proposed strategic investment and commercial collaboration. The company expects to invest up to $1,000,000, including $250,000 upfront to support commercialization and $750,000 upon execution of definitive agreements. Indicative terms include an $8,000,000 pre-money valuation, warrant coverage, a potential reseller arrangement, and a right for Brand Engagement Network to appoint one Accelevate board member at closing. The proposal remains subject to due diligence and definitive documentation; only confidentiality, exclusivity, diligence and certain other provisions are binding.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Letter agreement for proposed strategic investment and commercial collaboration
  • Counterparty: Accelevate Solutions
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 21 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Pursue strategic investment and commercial collaboration with reseller potential

Original SEC Filing: Brand Engagement Network Inc. [ BNAI ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

data.title: Diesel timespreads recover, May refiner spot sales stay upbeat
data.description:
Asia's diesel market activity was brisk as May refiner sales remained in full swing, though window spot acitivity stayed tepid while the backwardation in the swaps market firmed.

Some 10 or more spot cargoes have been sold by a handful of South Korea-based oil majors for May loading so far, several trade sources said, with more offers still surfacing.

Deals for the remaining May spot cargoes loading in the second half of the month were mostly at single-digit premiums, the sources added.

Meanwhile, for May, China-origin exports on a spot basis were still being discussed, though further details could not be confirmed.

Refining margins (GO10SGCKMc1) recouped most of previous sessions' losses, recovering to around $54 a barrel.

Cash differentials (GO10-SIN-DIF) also climbed back to around $18 a barrel, despite a lack of spot activity on the trading window.

More selling interest from northeast Asia surfaced, with at least one refiner selling their end-May requirements via a spot tender that closes tomorrow.

Regrade (JETREG10SGMc1) eased slightly to $16 a barrel, reflecting the firmer trading sentiment in gasoil paper markets.

SINGAPORE CASH DEALS

- No deal for gasoil or jet fuel

- U.S. crude oil inventories fell after three straight weeks of gains, while gasoline and distillate stocks also declined, market sources said, citing American Petroleum Institute figures on Tuesday.

- Middle distillates stocks held at Fujairah Oil Industry Zone rose slightly to 1.14 million barrels for the week ended April 20, according to industry information service S&P Global Commodity Insights.

REFINERY NEWS

- Japanese oil refineries slightly increased utilisation rates to 68.4% of designed capacity in the week to April 18, versus 67.8% a week earlier, Petroleum Association of Japan data showed on Wednesday.

NEWS

- Shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remained broadly halted on Tuesday with only three ships passing the waterway in the past 24 hours, shipping data showed.

- China will have to return to buying large volumes of oil within three weeks after being a big seller during the peak of the Iran supply crisis, the head of Mercuria, one of the world's largest energy traders, told the FT Commodities Summit.

- The European Union will provide guidance to airlines on how to handle issues such as airport slots, passenger rights and public service obligations in the event of jet fuel shortages because of the Iran war, the bloc's transport chief said on Tuesday.

- Oil prices jumped on Wednesday with Brent trading above $100 a barrel, erasing earlier losses following reports of gunfire attacks on at least three container ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

- The government of Australia's Queensland state on Wednesday said it would invest A$25 million ($18 million) in a renewable diesel project, casting the decision as the first step towards reducing its reliance on imported fuel.

data.title: ZAWYA: Reinsurance is the growth engine for digital insurance in Africa and the Middle East – EIRS
data.description:
• Middle East capital seen as key to unlocking Africa’s insurance growth
  • EIRS warns digital insurance expansion may outpace risk capacity in MEA

Dubai, UAE/Nairobi, Kenya – As digital insurance gains meaningful traction across Africa and the Middle East, a critical structural gap is emerging, masking a deeper structural risk and one that could potentially slow the sector’s momentum if left unaddressed.

This, analysts say, further highlights the growing need for robust, well-aligned reinsurance support to sustain growth. According to EIRS, the real constraint is no longer demand or distribution, but access to purpose-driven reinsurance capacity, particularly in high-volume, high-volatility segments such as motor insurance.

Africa landscape

In key markets like Kenya and South Africa, motor insurance remains a cornerstone of the industry. Yet profitability is under sustained pressure. Rising claims costs, fraud, and aggressive pricing have pushed loss ratios above sustainable levels for several insurers, forcing some to scale back underwriting or exit segments altogether.

At the same time, digital platforms are accelerating policy issuance and expanding access, often without a corresponding evolution in how risks are structured or transferred.

“Digital insurance is scaling faster than the risk frameworks that support it,” said Abhishek Jain, chief executive officer, EIRS Digital Insurance Ecosystem. “In several African markets, particularly in motor, we are seeing growth that is not adequately backed by reinsurance discipline. That creates systemic pressure. The opportunity is not just to grow, but to grow correctly.”

Across Africa, insurance penetration remains low, hovering around 3% to 3.5% of GDP, compared to a global average of 7%. This signals significant headroom for growth, particularly as mobile-first distribution models gain traction. However, it also underscores the need for stronger risk foundations as volumes increase.

Middle East focus

Across both regions, insurance penetration has remained largely flat below 3%, not due to lack of demand, but due to structural constraints in risk capacity, distribution, and reinsurance support. However, the Middle East, particularly hubs such as Dubai, remains a center for insurance capital and innovation. Markets like the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia have invested and continue to support digital transformation heavily, with insurers deploying AI, automation, and embedded insurance solutions to drive efficiency and scale.

Yet, despite these advancements, the link between Middle Eastern capital and Africa’s rapidly expanding risk landscape stays underdeveloped.

Bridging the gap

“Africa is often labelled high-risk, but in many cases, it is simply mispriced due to lack of local insight,” Abhishek added. “When you combine on-the-ground intelligence with structured reinsurance and access to capital from the Middle East, the risk becomes far more manageable, and far more attractive.”

Reinsurance, long viewed as a backend mechanism, is now being repositioned as a frontline enabler of growth. Without it, insurers face limits on how much risk they can absorb, particularly in sectors exposed to volatility, such as motor, trade credit, and infrastructure. As digital insurance continues its rapid expansion, the message is clear: technology may drive access, but reinsurance will determine sustainability.

About EIRS

EIRS, a subsidiary of ETG, is a global risk advisory and (re-)insurance broking firm dedicated to delivering holistic, data-driven risk solutions tailored for emerging markets across Africa and the Middle East. Founded in 2019 and operating across 11 African markets with strategic hubs in Dubai and London, EIRS combines deep regional insights with global insurance expertise. Its customer-centric offerings span traditional insurable risks (agriculture, aviation, marine & cargo, construction & engineering, hospitality, trade credit and more) as well as non-insurable risks such as supply-chain disruptions, credit volatility, market fluctuations, cyber exposure, and regulatory risk. Through its risk modelling approach, EIRS helps SMEs and enterprises navigate increasingly complex business environments with confidence, resilience and agility.

Press Contacts:

Marevak Consulting

Patrick Karanjah – patrick.karanjah@marevak-consulting.com

info@marevak.com

data.title: Key facts: BHP Q3 iron ore up 3%; China fines ban lifted, copper focus
data.description:
• BHP Q3 iron ore production 69.8Mt (100% basis), up 3% YoY from 67.8Mt. Maintained FY guidance 284–296Mt (100% basis). Q3 output amid disruptions from two tropical cyclones.1
  • BHP: 9-month copper output down 3% to 1,461 kt; iron ore up 2% to 197 Mt. Fiscal 2026 copper now expected in upper half of guidance. Samarco to hit top-end guidance; iron ore guidance unchanged.2
  • China's CMRG lifted its ban on BHP's Jimblebar fines after high-level Beijing talks; CMRG can resume USD purchases and accept held Jimblebar fines cargoes.3
  • BHP says seaborne iron ore demand will stay near current levels for several years, with modest China decline offset by gains in emerging markets and Europe recovery; global volumes broadly stable.4
  • BHP says copper is central to growth. CEO Mike Henry reports progress on copper projects, cites resilience amid market and cost pressures, and confirms July leadership handover to Brandon Craig.5

data.title: OpenAI briefs US agencies, Five Eyes on new cybersecurity product, Axios reports
data.description:
OpenAI has briefed U.S. federal agencies, state governments and Five Eyes member countries on the capabilities of its new cybersecurity product over the past week, Axios reported on Wednesday.

Cybersecurity is becoming a key battleground for AI labs such as OpenAI and Anthropic as their advanced AI models can both pose security risks and offer cyber defense capabilities, sparking interest from governments and enterprises.

OpenAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment. Reuters could not independently verify the report.

The ChatGPT-maker unveiled GPT-5.4-Cyber last week, a variant of its latest flagship model fine-tuned specifically ​for defensive cybersecurity work, following rival Anthropic's ‌announcement of advanced AI model Mythos.

OpenAI held an event in D.C. on Tuesday for about 50 cyber defense practitioners across the federal government to demo the capabilities of its new GPT-5.4-Cyber model, according to the report by Axios.

OpenAI is starting briefings with Five Eyes members this week to get them vetted and signed up to access the model, the report said.

The Five Eyes intelligence sharing network comprises the U.S., Britain, Canada, Australia and New Zealand.

Sam Altman-led OpenAI has said that GPT-5.4-Cyber would initially be rolled ​out on a limited basis to vetted ​security vendors, organizations and researchers because of its more permissive ‌design.

Earlier this month, rival Anthropic also announced a "Project Glasswing" initiative with major technology companies that lets partners preview the startup's unreleased model.

data.title: Rupee extends losing streak as Iran war jitters lift oil to $100
data.description:
By Jaspreet Kalra

The Indian rupee fell for the third consecutive session on Wednesday, as optimism about an end to the Iran war remained muted despite the U.S. indefinitely extending a ceasefire.

With reports of gunfire attacks on container ships in the Strait of Hormuz, Brent crude oil prices rose above $100 a barrel and pegged back regional stocks, including a 0.9% drop in India's benchmark Nifty 50 index.

The rupee closed at 93.7950, down 0.3% from the previous close and hovering near its weakest level in three weeks.

The rupee managed to avoid steeper losses on the day on account of likely intervention by the central bank, traders said.

Oil-sensitive Asian currencies were on the defensive with the Philippine peso and Indonesian rupiah enduring losses, while the Chinese yuan, an overall outperformer in the region, held steady.

"For now, the financial markets remain in limbo, awaiting news on whether there will be progress or whether another wave of military attacks lies ahead," MUFG said in a note.

"We are surely on the cusp of another surge in crude oil prices if this ceasefire does not hold, and that will most likely have a more notable knock-on impact on risk assets."

The future trajectory of the rupee, though, traders and analysts say, will depend on how high oil prices remain and for how long.

An extended energy shock is likely to keep the rupee under strain, and it "wouldn't be a surprise," if the central bank takes additional measures to support the currency, a trader at a Singapore-based hedge fund said.

Despite weakness in the local currency, dollar-rupee forward premiums retreated in afternoon trading from intraday highs, with traders pointing to forward dollar sales from both foreign and state-run lenders.

data.title: Exxon eyes sale of Hong Kong gas stations for up to $600 million, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
U.S. energy major Exxon Mobil is considering a sale of its gas stations in Hong Kong and may seek a valuation of $500 million to $600 million for the assets, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Exxon did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

The oil and gas producer provides several products and services to customers in Hong Kong through an extensive network of service stations operating under the Esso brand.

Exxon's first service station in Hong Kong opened in 1926 in Kowloon. The firm now sells fuel through a network of about 41 Esso-branded service stations to motorists in Hong Kong.

data.title: First Northern Community Bancorp to List on Nasdaq Capital Market; Trading to Transition April 24
data.description:
First Northern Community Bancorp announced Nasdaq Capital Market approval and expects trading to transition from OTCQX on April 24, 2026.

Key Highlights:

  • Nasdaq approved First Northern Community Bancorp’s common stock for listing on The Nasdaq Capital Market.
  • Shares will transition from OTCQX to Nasdaq effective at market open on April 24, 2026, pending listing requirements.
  • Common stock will continue trading on OTCQX through end of trading April 23, 2026.
  • Shares will trade on Nasdaq under the existing ticker symbol FNRN; shareholders need take no action.

Original SEC Filing: FIRST NORTHERN COMMUNITY BANCORP [ FNRN ] - 8-K - Apr. 21, 2026
